commit.message:
feat: JUL → API Access Bridge — work-credit closed loop

JUL is now a work-credit system: mine → earn → burn → compute access.
No AMM, no cash conversion. Work in, access out.

mining.js: Treasury state (totalBurned, dailyBurned, tips[]),
burnJUL(), tipJUL(), getDailyBurned(), getTreasuryStats().

compute-economics.js: Dynamic pool via getEffectivePool() =
BASE_POOL + (dailyJulBurned × 1000). Auto-burn in checkBudget()
when user exhausts free budget but has JUL balance.

index.js: /tip burns JUL to expand pool for everyone,
/balance shows bridge stats + effective pool,
/economy shows base + JUL bonus breakdown + treasury.

1 JUL burned = 1,000 extra API tokens. Symmetric ratio —
no premium for generosity. Will subsidizes the base pool.
Burn rate = transparent demand signal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(coolify): regenerate weak credentials and harden probes

Root cause: Cuemby Cloud injects short placeholder passwords via
cloud-init for all PARAM_* fields. The pre-install hook only checked
for empty/placeholder values, so it accepted short strings as valid.
Laravel APP_KEY requires "base64:<32-bytes>" format for AES-256-CBC,
causing HTTP 500 on every request.

Changes:
- Add _needs_password() helper that also rejects values < 16 chars
- APP_KEY: always regenerate unless it starts with "base64:"
- DB/Redis/Pusher passwords: regenerate if shorter than 16 chars
- Liveness probe: switch to tcpSocket (tolerates HTTP 500 during
  Laravel migration/seeder phase, same as startupProbe)
- Readiness probe: increase failureThreshold to 6 (60s grace period
  for migrations before marking pod as not-ready)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Stream agent thinking to chat with collapsible tool activity

- Convert /api/chat from JSON response to NDJSON streaming, emitting
  events as they happen: tool_call, tool_result, schema_update, error,
  text, done
- useChat consumes the stream in real-time, accumulating ThinkingSteps
- New ThinkingBlock component shows live tool activity during processing
  (auto-expanded with bouncing dots), then collapses to summary ("3
  tools used") once the final response arrives. Click to expand/collapse.
- Tool results show green checkmarks for success, red X for failures
- Errors surfaced inline with red highlighting
- Page updates mid-loop as each tool executes (not just at the end)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(skills): ecosystem consistency - versions, naming, frontmatter (#65)

* feat(syner): add planning mode for autonomous work

Add planning.md as built-in mode for breaking down complex work:
- Agnostic: no dependency on GitHub, labels, or any platform
- Classification: 0 items, 1 item, N items, ambiguous, error
- Clarity criteria: target, action, verification
- Checklist format with optional metadata
- Ambiguity handling (preserves info, excludes from plan)

Triggers: explicit user request or analysis with multiple findings.
Output: markdown checklist (where it goes depends on context).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(syner): implement autonomous work loop

Part 2 of planning mode implementation:

Planning Mode:
- Updated output format: Target → Context → Action → Verify
- Ready-to-use instruction format for any context

GitHub Integration (packages/github/planning.md):
- Label protocol: needs-planning → planning, claude → execution
- Issue-based state machine for autonomous loop
- PR creation as feedback point

Workflows:
- claude.yml: now triggers on both claude and needs-planning labels
- skill-review.yml: creates issues with needs-planning label
- daily-standup.yml: creates issues with needs-planning label

The loop:
1. Workflow creates issue + [needs-planning]
2. Syner plans → checklist + [claude]
3. Syner executes item → PR
4. Human approves PR (only touchpoint)
5. Repeat until done

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(autonomous): complete autonomous work loop with Mike model

- Add planning mode prompt (.github/prompts/planning.md)
- Add dynamic mode detection in claude.yml (planning vs execution)
- Add auto-PR creation post-step in claude.yml
- Add next.yml for feedback loop (re-trigger on PR merge)
- Update packages/github/planning.md with new architecture

Model: User Story + Acceptance Criteria (Mike)
- One issue with checklist, not subtask issues
- Each checklist item = one PR
- Loop continues until all items checked

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bulletproof recovery for stale SW cache crashes

- Script onerror now directly triggers _kernelRecover() instead of just
  setting a flag (eliminates 20s wait on broken deploys)
- Error handler checks e.target.src for script load failures, not just
  e.filename (which is undefined for load errors)
- SW unregister + cache clear now properly awaited with Promise.all
  before reloading (fixes race condition where reload happened before
  SW was fully unregistered)
- Splash timeout reduced from 20s to 8s (still safe for slow 3G)
- Added _recovering debounce to prevent multiple simultaneous recoveries
- "Having trouble loading" text softened to "Updating — one moment"

commit.message:
Gold Plating Calculator - full Leptos web app

Step-by-step wizard guiding jewelers through the Gold Solutions Plating
silver-to-gold tank plating process. Built with Rust/Leptos 0.8 (Axum SSR
+ WASM hydration).

Features:
- 8-step wizard with progress bar (pretreatment through finish)

feat: repo lightweight — browsers auto-descargados por iniciar.bat

descargan automáticamente al ejecutar iniciar.bat por primera vez.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Passthrough on invalid config instead of blocking all tools (#9)

* fix: Output ask decision instead of crashing on invalid config

When config fails to load, output an "ask" decision with the error
as the reason instead of exiting non-zero. This surfaces the config
error to the user rather than silently blocking all tools.

Fixes #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Passthrough on invalid config instead of blocking all tools

When config fails to load, log the error to stderr and exit 0 with
no stdout (passthrough) instead of exiting non-zero which blocks
all tool use. The user loses their custom rules but tools keep working.

Fixes #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Q10 select entity support

Researched python-roborock library and found Q10 API methods:
- vacuum.set_clean_mode(YXCleanType) - Sets cleaning mode
- vacuum.set_fan_level(YXFanLevel) - Sets fan speed
- command.send(B01_Q10_DP.WATER_LEVEL, YXWaterLevel) - Sets water level

Added Q10-specific select descriptions:
- Clean mode (both work, only sweep, only mop)
- Fan level (close, quiet, normal, strong, max, super)
- Water level (close, low, middle, high)

Q10 devices now have full control capabilities!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: hass-roborock
repository.description: Extended Roborock integration for Home Assistant with B01 Q10 protocol support

commit.message:
Fix zombie audits on cancel and add schedule export/import

Zombie fix:
- processAuditQueue now sets session._cancelledExternally before startAudit
- onUpdate callback ignores all updates once a session is cancelled or errored,
  preventing late async callbacks from overwriting the cancelled/error status
- finally block skips runningAudits decrement when cancel endpoint already
  released the slot, eliminating double-release accounting errors
- DELETE /api/audit/:id sets _cancelledExternally before marking session as error
- Fix misleading timeout message (was '25 minutes', is actually 240 minutes)

Export/Import:
- GET /api/scheduler/schedules/export — downloads all schedules as JSON
- POST /api/scheduler/schedules/import — restores from JSON backup (merges by ID)
- Export and Import buttons added to scheduler dashboard header
